name: ECS on Fargate
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and security adoption pack for Amazon ECS services running on AWS Fargate.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Complete visibility into service performance, runtime health, request flow, and customer-facing impact.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Trace requests across ALB, ECS tasks, and downstream services so teams can explain failures and latency.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure availability, latency, error rate, and deployment success against service-level objectives.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Validate least privilege, image hygiene, network exposure, secrets handling, and auditability.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously test health, deployment quality, runtime posture, and operational readiness.

sections.name: Service Overview
sections.purpose: Cluster, service, desired task count, running task count, deployment state, and recent events.

sections.name: Compute Saturation
sections.purpose: CPU utilization, memory utilization, task restarts, and scaling pressure.

sections.name: Traffic and Availability
sections.purpose: ALB request count, target response time, HTTP 4xx/5xx, target health, and availability indicators.

sections.name: Application Errors
sections.purpose: CloudWatch log error trends, exception signatures, and failed dependency calls.

sections.name: Traces and Service Map
sections.purpose: OpenTelemetry or X-Ray traces showing request path, downstream latency, and fault locations.

sections.name: Security Posture
sections.purpose: Security Hub findings, GuardDuty findings, ECR scan status, IAM drift, and public exposure checks.

sections.name: Cost and Efficiency
sections.purpose: Fargate CPU and memory allocation, scaling utilization, idle capacity, and rightsizing signals.

metrics.name: CPUUtilization
metrics.why: Detect saturation, under-provisioning, and scaling triggers.

metrics.name: MemoryUtilization
metrics.why: Detect memory pressure and out-of-memory risk.

metrics.name: RunningTaskCount
metrics.why: Confirm the service is actually running the desired number of tasks.

metrics.name: TargetResponseTime
metrics.why: Measure user-facing latency through the load balancer.

metrics.name: UnHealthyHostCount
metrics.why: Detect failed or unreachable ECS tasks behind the target group.

log_queries.name: Application errors by message
log_queries.intent: Find the most frequent error patterns in ECS application logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /(?i)(error|exception|failed)/ | stats count() by @message | sort count desc | limit 20

log_queries.name: Slow request signatures
log_queries.intent: Find slow operations when logs include duration fields.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message, durationMs | filter durationMs > 1000 | sort durationMs desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: Task startup failures
log_queries.intent: Find container startup and dependency initialization failures.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /(?i)(startup|init|timeout|connection refused|permission denied)/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

alarms.name: ECSHighCPU
alarms.action: Notify service owner and evaluate scale-out policy.

alarms.name: ECSHighMemory
alarms.action: Notify service owner and check memory leaks or task sizing.

alarms.name: ALBTarget5xxSpike
alarms.condition: Target 5xx count greater than threshold for 5 minutes
alarms.action: Start incident triage with logs and traces.

alarms.name: ALBUnhealthyTargets
alarms.condition: One or more unhealthy targets for 5 minutes
alarms.action: Check ECS task health checks, container port, and deployment state.

alarms.name: ECSRunningTaskMismatch
alarms.condition: Running task count is below desired task count
alarms.action: Investigate placement, image pull, task role, networking, or capacity issues.

security_controls.name: Task role least privilege
security_controls.evidence: IAM policy attached to the task role grants only required service actions.

security_controls.name: Private task networking
security_controls.evidence: ECS tasks run in private subnets where possible, with ingress only from the ALB security group.

security_controls.name: Container image scanning
security_controls.evidence: ECR scan findings are reviewed and critical vulnerabilities block production deployment.

security_controls.name: Centralized audit trail
security_controls.evidence: CloudTrail captures ECS, IAM, ECR, and deployment API activity.

security_controls.name: Runtime threat detection
security_controls.evidence: GuardDuty findings are enabled and routed to Security Hub.

security_controls.name: Configuration compliance
security_controls.evidence: AWS Config evaluates security groups, encryption, logging, and public exposure controls.

adoption_plan: Deploy the chatbot and ECS service-pack API to a non-production AWS account.
Enable ECS Container Insights on the cluster.
Instrument the application with OpenTelemetry or X-Ray tracing.
Create the ECS Fargate CloudWatch dashboard from the approved service-pack template.
Create CPU, memory, ALB 5xx, target health, and task mismatch alarms.
Connect alarms to the organization's incident channel or ticketing flow.
Enable Security Hub, AWS Config, GuardDuty, CloudTrail, and ECR image scanning evidence checks.
Review the dashboard with application, platform, and security owners.
Promote the pack as the default ECS monitoring standard for new services.
